question: Please indicate, for each of the following, how important it is in your life.
Here is a list of qualities that children can be encouraged to learn at home. Which, if any, do you consider to be especially important?
sub_questions: Tolerance and respect for other people

question: Here are various groups of people. Could you please tick any that you would not like to have as neighbours?
sub_questions: People who speak a different language

question: People talk about the changing roles of men and women today. For each of the following statements, please indicate how much you agree or disagree.
sub_questions: One of my main goals in life has been to make my parents proud
A preschool child suffers if their mother works
Men make better political leaders than women do
A university education is more important for a boy than a girl
Men make better business executives than women do
Being a housewife is just as fulfilling as working for pay

question: Do you agree or disagree with the following statements?
sub_questions: When jobs are scarce, men should have more right to a job than women
When jobs are scarce, employers should give priority to New Zealanders over immigrants
If a woman earns more than her husband, it is almost certain to cause problems

question: How much do you agree or disagree with the following statements?
sub_questions: Homosexual couples are as good parents as other couples
It is a duty towards society to have children
It is children's duty to take care of ill parents
People who don't work turn lazy
Work is a duty towards society
Work should always come first, even if it means less spare time

question: Listed below are three basic kinds of attitudes concerning the society we live in. Please choose the one that best describes your own opinion.
word_response_types: The entire way our society is organised must be radically changed by revolutionary action
Our society must be gradually improved by reforms
Our present society must be valiantly defended against all subversive forces

question: Listed below are various changes in our way of life that might take place in the near future. Please indicate whether you think each would be a good thing, a bad thing, or don't you mind?
sub_questions: More emphasis on the development of technology

question: Taking all things together, would you say you are:
All in all, how would you describe your state of health these days? Would you say it is:

Some people feel they have completely free choice and control over their lives, while other people feel that what they do has no real effect on what happens to them. Please use this scale where 1 means "none at all" and 10 means "a great deal" to indicate how much freedom of choice and control you feel you have over the way your life turns out.
All things considered, how satisfied are you with your life as a whole these days? Please use this scale where 1 means you are "completely dissatisfied" and 10 means you are "completely satisfied".
How satisfied are you with the financial situation of your household? Please use this scale where 1 means "completely dissatisfied" and 10 means "completely satisfied".
In the last 12 months, how often have you or your family:
sub_questions: Gone without enough food to eat
Felt unsafe from crime in your own home
Gone without needed medicine or medical treatment that you needed
Gone without a safe shelter over your head

question: Comparing your standard of living with your parents' standard of living when they were about your age, would you say that you are better off, worse off, or about the same?
Generally speaking, would you say that most people can be trusted or that you need to be very careful in dealing with people?
Here are some questions about how much you trust people from various groups. Please indicate how much you trust...
sub_questions: People you meet for the first time

question: Here are the names of a number of organisations. For each one, please indicate how much confidence you have in them: is it a great deal of confidence, quite a lot of confidence, not very much confidence or none at all?

People sometimes say that international organisations should prioritise improving people's lives, even if this may imply that decisions are not made democratically. What do you think international organisations should prioritise, being effective or being democratic? If your views are somewhat mixed, choose the appropriate number in between. (1 = Being effective, 10 = Being democratic)
Five countries have permanent seats on the Security Council of the United Nations. Which one of the following is not a member?
sub_questions: Countries with permanent seats on UN Security Council

question: Where are the headquarters of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) located?
sub_questions: Where are the headquarters of the IMF located?

question: Which one of the following problems does the organisation, Amnesty International, deal with?
sub_questions: Which problem does Amnesty International deal with?

question: Listed below are different types of voluntary organisations. For each, are you an active member, an inactive member, or don't you belong?
How would you place your views on this scale? 1 means you agree completely with the statement on the left; 10 means you agree completely with the statement on the right.
sub_questions: Incomes should be made more equal vs There should be greater incentives for individual effort
Private ownership should be increased vs Government ownership should be increased
Government should take more responsibility vs People should take more responsibility
Competition is good vs Competition is harmful
Hard work brings a better life vs Hard work doesn't bring success

question: Here are two statements people sometimes make when discussing the environment and economic growth. Which one comes closest to your own point of view?
word_response_types: Protecting environment should be given priority
Economic growth and creating jobs should be top priority

question: How would you place your views on corruption in New Zealand on a 10-point scale where 1 means "there is no corruption in New Zealand" and 10 means "there is abundant corruption in New Zealand"?
Among the following groups of people, how many do you believe are involved in corruption?

How often do you think ordinary people like yourself or people from your neighbourhood have to pay a bribe, give a gift or do a favour to these people in order to get the services needed? Does it happen never, rarely, frequently or always?
How strongly do you agree or disagree with the following statement: "On the whole, women are less corrupt than men"?

How high is the risk in New Zealand of being held accountable for giving or receiving a bribe, gift or favour in return for public service? Please indicate your opinion on the 10 point scale below, where 1 means "no risk at all" and 10 means "very high risk".
How would you evaluate the impact of immigrants on the development of New Zealand?
What have been the effects of immigration on the development of New Zealand? For each of the following statements, please indicate whether you agree or disagree with it.
sub_questions: Gives asylum to political refugees who are persecuted elsewhere
Offers people from poorer countries a better living

question: How about people from other countries coming here to work. Which one of the following do you think the government should do?
word_response_types: Let anyone come who wants to
Let people come as long as there are jobs available
Place strict limits on the number who can come here
Prohibit people coming here from other countries

question: Please tell us how secure you feel these days in your neighbourhood?
How frequently do the following things occur in your neighbourhood?
sub_questions: Police or military interference with people's private life

question: Which of the following things have you done for reasons of security?
sub_questions: Preferred not to go out at night
Carried a knife, gun or other weapon

question: Have you or your family been the victim of a crime during the past year?
sub_questions: Respondent was victim of a crime
Respondent's family was victim of a crime

question: Were you or your family a victim of a crime during the past year?
To what degree are you worried about the following situations?
sub_questions: Losing my job or not finding a job
Not being able to give my children a good education

question: Most people consider both freedom and equality to be important, but if you had to choose between them, which one would you consider most important?
Most people consider both freedom and security to be important, but if you had to choose between them, which one would you consider most important?
Of course, we all hope there will not be another war, but if it were to come to that, would you be willing to fight for your country?

People sometimes talk about what the aims of this country should be for the next ten years. Listed below are some of the goals which different people would give top priority. Would you please say which one of these you, yourself, consider the most important? And which would be the next most important?
word_response_types: A high level of economic growth
People have more say at work and in their communities
Making cities and countryside more beautiful

question: If you had to choose, which one of the things on this card would you say is most important? And which would be the next most important?
word_response_types: Giving people more say in important government decisions

question: Here is another list. In your opinion, which one of these is most important? And which would be the next most important?
word_response_types: Progress toward a less impersonal and more humane society
Progress toward a society where ideas count more than money

question: Here are some statements about science and technology. How much do you agree or disagree with each of these statements? For these questions, a 1 means that you "completely disagree" and a 10 means that you "completely agree."
sub_questions: Science and technology are making our lives healthier, easier, and more comfortable
Because of science and technology, there will be more opportunities for the next generation
We depend too much on science and not enough on faith
One of the bad effects of science is that it breaks down people's ideas of right and wrong
It is not important for me to know about science in my daily life
The world is better off because of science and technology

question: How important is God in your life? Please use this scale to indicate your strength of belief: 1 means not at all important and 10 means very important.
Which, if any, of the following do you believe in?
How much do you agree or disagree with the following statements?
sub_questions: Whenever science and religion conflict, religion is always right
The only acceptable religion is my religion

question: Apart from weddings, funerals and christenings, about how often do you attend religious services these days?
Apart from weddings and funerals, about how often do you pray?
Independently of whether you go to religious services or not, would you say you are:
Meaning of religion: To follow religious norms and ceremonies vs To do good to other people
Meaning of religion: To make sense of life after death vs To make sense of life in this world
word_response_types: Make sense of life after death
Make sense of life in this world

question: How much do you agree or disagree with the statement that nowadays one often has trouble deciding which moral rules are the right ones to follow? (1 = Completely agree, 10 = Completely disagree)
Please indicate for each of the following statements whether you think it can always be justified, never be justified, or something in between. (1 = Never justifiable, 10 = Always justifiable)
sub_questions: Claiming government benefits to which you are not entitled
Avoiding a fare on public transport
Cheating on taxes if you have a chance
Buying something you knew was stolen
Someone accepting a bribe in the course of their duties
For a man to beat his wife
Terrorism as a political, ideological, or religious means

question: Do you think that the New Zealand Government should or should not have the right to do each of the following?
sub_questions: Keep people under video surveillance in public areas
Monitor all emails and any other information exchanged on the internet
Collect information about anyone living in New Zealand without their knowledge
word_response_types: Probably should not have the right
Definitely should not have the right

question: How interested would you say you are in politics?
When you get together with friends, would you say you discuss political matters frequently, occasionally or never?
How often do you use each of the following information sources?

Here are some different forms of political action that people can take. For each one, please indicate whether you have actually done any of these things, whether you might do it or would never, under any circumstances, do it.
For each social activism action, have you done it, might do it, or would never do it?

Listed below are some other forms of political action people can take using internet and social media tools like Facebook, Twitter, etc. For each one please indicate whether you have done any of these things, whether you might do it, would never under any circumstances do it.
sub_questions: Searching information about politics and political events
Encouraging other people to take any form of political action

question: When elections take place, do you always vote, usually vote, or never vote? Please tell us separately how often you vote at a local level, and at the national level.
If there were a national election tomorrow which party would you vote for? And which Party would be your second choice?
In your view how often do each of the following things occur in New Zealand's elections?
sub_questions: Opposition candidates are prevented from running
TV news favours the governing party
Journalists provide fair coverage of elections
Voters are threatened with violence at the polls
Voters are offered a genuine choice in the elections
Women have equal opportunities to run for office

question: Some people think that having honest elections makes a lot of difference in their lives; other people think that it does not matter much. How important would you say is having honest elections for you?
How much does the political system allow you to have a say?

Listed below are various types of political systems. Please think about each one as a way of governing this country. For each one, would you say it is a very good, fairly good, fairly bad or very bad way of governing this country?
sub_questions: Having a strong leader who does not have to bother with parliament and elections
Having experts, not government, make decisions according to what they think is best for the country
Having a system governed by religious law in which there are no political parties or elections

question: In political matters, people talk of "the left" and "the right". How would you place your views on this scale, generally speaking? (1 = Left, 10 = Right)

Many things are desirable, but not all of them are essential characteristics of democracy. Please tell us, for each of the following things how essential you think it is as a characteristic of democracy? Use this scale where 1 means "not at all an essential characteristic of democracy" and 10 means it definitely is "an essential characteristic of democracy".
sub_questions: Governments tax the rich and subsidize the poor
Religious authorities ultimately interpret the laws
People choose their leaders in free elections
People receive state aid for unemployment
The army takes over when government is incompetent
Civil rights protect people from state oppression
The state makes people's incomes equal
Women have the same rights as men

question: How important is it for you to live in a country that is governed democratically? On this scale where 1 means it is "not at all important" and 10 means "absolutely important" what position would you choose?

And how democratically is this country being governed today? Again using a scale from 1 to 10, where 1 means that it is "not at all democratic" and 10 means that it is "completely democratic," what position would you choose?
How satisfied are you with how the political system is functioning in New Zealand these days? On the scale from 1 to 10 below, 1 means not satisfied at all and 10 means completely satisfied.
How much respect is there for individual human rights nowadays in New Zealand? Do you feel there is:
How proud are you to be a New Zealander?
People have different views about themselves and how they relate to the world. Please tell us how close you feel to...
sub_questions: Your village, town or city where you live
Your region of the country where you live

question: Are you:
What year were you born in?
Age
Are you and your parents immigrants to New Zealand?
word_response_types: I am born in this country

question: Can you please tell us where you and your parents were born?
Are you a citizen of New Zealand?
Including yourself, how many people – including children – live here regularly as members of this household? (1 to 10 or more)
Do you live with your parents or your parents-in-law?
What language do you normally speak at home?
Which of the following categories describe your ethnic origin?
Have you had any children? If YES, how many? (0 to 8 or more)
Which of these categories describe your employment status? Then please tell us about the employment status of your spouse or partner.
To which occupational group do you belong? If you have more than one job, tell us about the main job. Then please tell us about the occupational group of your spouse/partner, and then your father.

Are you working for the government or a public institution, for private business or industry, or for a private non-profit organization? If you are not currently working, tell us about your major kind of work in the past!
Are you the chief wage earner in your household?
During the past year, did your family (or you, yourself, if you live independently):
People sometimes describe themselves as belonging to the working class, the middle class, or the upper or lower class. Would you describe yourself as belonging to the:

Scale of incomes – On this card is a scale of incomes on which 1 indicates the lowest income group and 10 the highest income group in New Zealand. Please specify the appropriate number, counting all wages, salaries, pensions and other incomes. (1 = Lower step, 10 = Upper step)
What is your religion?
Highest formal education level (ISCED coding)
Which one of these categories best describes the highest level of formal education you have had?
word_response_types: Secondary school for up to 3 years
Secondary school for 4 years or more
Some university, wananga, polytechnic or other tertiary
